posts.text: New poll shows voters overwhelmingly oppose using force on Cuba, just 8% of Trump voters and 7% of all support it

Cuban president promises the country will forcefully resist if attacked.


Miguel Díaz-Canel Bermúdez (@DiazCanelB)

#EEUU amenaza públicamente a #Cuba, casi a diario, con derrocar por la fuerza el orden constitucional. Y usa un indignante pretexto: las duras limitaciones de la debilitada economía que ellos han agredido y pretendido aislar hace más de seis décadas.

Pretenden y anuncian planes para adueñarse del país, de sus recursos, de las propiedades y hasta de la misma economía que buscan as

They are new and probably still smarting from losing the @NidaAllam race to AI and AIPAC.

Everybody who jumps into electoral politics from tech or finance thinks there’s a quantitative answer they can see that others missed. But there’s still a feel to it, an art that requires humility. Everyone learns that the hard way.

Huge miss here by American Priorities, the new anti-genocide PAC with deep pockets. They believed Junaid Ahmed couldn’t win and resisted pressure to support him. Results show they could’ve made a decisive difference. AIPAC almost never holds back, and didn’t here, and so notched the W.

AIPAC’s candidate is in a distant third in this race despite outspending the field by millions. Let’s see how Josh is analyzing it…


Josh Kraushaar (@JoshKraushaar)

Abughazaleh is getting crushed in the Cook and Lake County suburbs. 

Evidence that Mamdani style leftism only has purchase in deep-blue cities and college towns.



— https://nitter.net/JoshKraushaar/status/2034071202080211289#m

You cannot consolidate the progressive vote today while saber-rattling war with China. That era is over.


ettingermentum (@ettingermentum)

Wow. Infuriating. We all know whose fault this is for not dropping out. That's right: Laura Fine for not allowing Kat to consolidate the pro-war vote.





— https://nitter.net/ettingermentum/status/2034084801364324388#m
AIPAC spent everything it could to boost Conyears-Ervin. Ouch.

Who is flexing? Does being on this site just automatically turn you into an asshole? Where’s this shot coming from? “My bartender” is a very common term. 

I actually knew/know the other bartender Ned much better, Graham was his backup. And this was like 15-20 years ago.

As Israel Calls for an Uprising in Iran, Basij Militias Vow to Crush Opposition to the State (by Drop Site News). Israel assassinated the head of the Basij and has been bombing its checkpoints across Iran.

The Iran War Rages On: Israeli Assassination Strikes, Steve Witkoff's Texts, and the Mounting Storm in the Strait of Hormuz (by Drop Site News). Jeremy Scahill and Ryan Grim discuss the latest with Foad Izadi and and Emran Feroz.
Israel says it killed top Iranian security official; Pakistan bombing kills hundreds in Kabul; Cuba hit by blackout amid Trump blockade (by Drop Site News)
As the Gulf Burns, Political Uncertainty Looms Over Iraq (by Drop Site News). Nouri al-Maliki, the former prime minister, is grooming himself for another stint in power as war consumes Iraq.

Israel Killed Over a Dozen Lebanese Paramedics in Three Days, Now Claiming That Ambulances Are “Hezbollah” Targets (by Lylla Younes). Among the most targeted groups of Israel’s expanded war on Lebanon have been medical workers. Hezbollah’s Islamic Health Authority has been the hardest hit.

Iranian Officials Say They Have Been Ignoring Witkoff's Private Requests to Talk (by Jeremy Scahill). Trump's special envoy has been texting Iran’s foreign minister asking to start talks. Tehran says the war will end only when Iran believes it has established long-term deterrence.
Trump struggles to secure Strait of Hormuz; Israel launches ground invasion of Lebanon; North Korea conducts new missile tests (by Drop Site News)
15,000 targets struck in Iran, Hegseth says; Lebanon is near a “breaking point”; U.S. temporarily lifts Russian oil sanctions (by Drop Site News)

Behind the Bombs, New Details Emerge on Iran’s Infiltration of Israel (by Jeremy Scahill). As arrests grow in Israel, internal Iranian intelligence materials reviewed by Drop Site shed light on Tehran’s clandestine attempts to turn Israeli citizens into spies.
UN says 3.2 million displaced in Iran; 40+ senators press Hegseth over Iran school bombing; Drone strikes in Sudan kill dozens of civilians (by Drop Site News)

Rescue Efforts in Tehran After a Triple Strike Hit Apartment Buildings, Killing 40 (by mahmoud aslan). “Every time we lifted a stone, we increased our sense that we might succeed in saving a life, or that we might find someone who could not be saved.”

NEW POLL: Majority of Americans Believe Trump Launched Iran War to Cover Up Epstein Scandal. A new Drop Site/Zeteo/Data For Progress survey also finds likely American voters are split on whether Trump is more responsive to the American people or to Israel.
Over 1,000 children injured in Iran; Unprecedented 400M barrels of oil reserves slated for release; Death and displacement mount in Lebanon amid Israeli assault (by Drop Site News)

In Tehran, Iranians Struggle to Breathe After Israeli Oil Facility Strikes (by Ariya Farahmand). "By the time we finally packed our bags and locked the door, our fingernails were caked in chemical grime, and our lungs were burning just from breathing inside our own living room."

Trump Might Want to End the War. Iran Won’t Do It on His Terms. (by Jeremy Scahill). Iran says the U.S. and Israel underestimated its military capacity and will to fight, as Trump careens toward a quagmire.
Trump went to war to distract from his JE scandal, according to a majority of Americans in a new survey we commissioned.
